text:
King's College Hospital 

Department of Neurosciences 

Clinic Date: 02 February 2022 

Dr Priya Menon
Consultant Neurologist  
King's College Hospital  
Department of Neurosciences, King's College Hospital, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr Priya Menon
Consultant Neurologist,  

Daniel Carter, DOB: 14-03-1986, Hospital No: K732518 NHS No. 9076123481  
12 Forge Lane, Bromley, BR2 8ST

Thank you for referring this gentleman, whom I reviewed in clinic today. He works in metal fabrication and welding and reports intermittent exposure to arc-light at work; he confirms that he uses a full-face protective visor as standard and adheres to workplace safety procedures. He was calm and cooperative, and we were able to take a clear history.

He describes his habitual events as follows: there is a brief prodrome of tightness in the chest and a rising internal anxiety, followed by loss of awareness with stiffening and irregular shaking of the limbs, sometimes with a cry at onset. Tongue-biting has occurred on two occasions and there is urinary incontinence about a third of the time. He is typically confused afterwards for 30–45 minutes and feels exhausted for the remainder of the day. He and his partner have kept a diary over the past year, and the spells are occurring roughly once every couple of months. There are no reliable triggers; he has not noticed any clear relationship to welding shifts or arc-light exposure, and he does not use alcohol or recreational drugs. Sleep is often fragmented due to early starts, but he is trying to maintain regular hours.

Neurological examination today was non-focal. There are no reported nocturnal injuries or bed disarray suggestive of frequent night-time events. He drives only for work-site access within private premises and states he has not driven on public roads since the onset of these episodes; we discussed ongoing DVLA obligations.

We talked through ongoing documentation of episodes and workplace risk. He confirms that his employer accommodates rest breaks and that spotters are present during hot-work tasks. He remains willing to attend for further investigations if required. We will continue to gather corroborative information from witnesses at work and from his partner. I have requested prior imaging and EEG from the local hospital for review and will arrange routine bloods including metabolic profile.

I will update you once the ancillary results are available and will review him again in four months, sooner if the event pattern changes from the current cadence of about one every two months.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: the spells are occurring roughly once every couple of months... the current cadence of about one every two months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states the episodes are occurring roughly once every couple of months and later reiterates the current cadence of about one every two months. This indicates a frequency of 1 seizure per 2 months. Convert to allowed format: '1 per 2 month'.
